prompt: Two images. IMAGE 1 is a scene: a woman standing in a Milos lane in a navy-and-white pinstripe tiered maxi dress. IMAGE 2 is the SPECIFIC PERSON whose face must appear. Replace the woman's FACE and HAIR so she clearly and unmistakably becomes the IMAGE 2 woman and NO LONGER resembles the original IMAGE 1 woman: her fuller, slightly rounder face shape, warm golden-olive skin with light freckles across nose and cheeks, full lips, strong defined dark eyebrows, warm brown eyes, and long warm dark-brown SOFTLY WAVED hair (keep her natural soft waves and warm dark-brown tone). The final face must look like a clearly different person from the original. PROPORTION LOCK: keep the head at the SAME SIZE and position as the head already in IMAGE 1; do NOT enlarge the head or hair; the figure must stay about seven and a half heads tall with a correctly small head relative to the body. Keep the navy pinstripe dress and every stripe/gather/tier/hem, her body, pose, hands, sandals, the Milos setting, and the warm late-afternoon light and shadows identical. Photorealistic; light the new face with the same warm side light.
